title: Dubliners
author: Joyce, James
summary: Dubliners is a collection of fifteen short stories by James Joyce portraying everyday life in Dublin through the experiences of ordinary characters across youth, adolescence, maturity, and public life. The stories—beginning with 'The Sisters' and ending with 'The Dead'—trace paralysis, religious influence, stalled aspirations, and social rituals. Joyce renders scenes of domestic interiors, social gatherings, and inner consciousness with precise realist detail and ironic distance. Recurring motifs include confinement, spiritual and emotional paralysis, failed communication, and yearning for escape. The book’s meticulous language and psychological acuity illuminate the moral and cultural stagnation of Dublin at the turn of the century.

characters.name: Narrator (the boy)
characters.personality: introspective, curious, impressionable; observant of religious and domestic detail
characters.lifespan: survives the events of the story; remains in Dublin (story ends with him attending funeral and reflecting)

characters.name: Father Flynn (Rev. James Flynn)
characters.personality: eccentric, learned, religiously observant, physically paralysed and enigmatic

characters.name: Old Cotter
characters.lifespan: alive during the story; social commentator who criticises clerical influence

characters.name: Aunt (the narrator's aunt)
characters.lifespan: alive; attends the funeral and comforts family

characters.name: Uncle (addressed as Jack by Cotter)
characters.personality: earthy, pragmatic, fond of health remedies and exercises, conversational
characters.lifespan: alive; present at dinner and discussion

characters.name: Nannie
characters.personality: dutiful, humble, steady in household tasks
characters.lifespan: alive; assists with funeral arrangements and serving

characters.name: Eliza (Miss Flynn)
characters.personality: grieving, practical, loyal to her brother, plain-speaking
characters.lifespan: alive; presides over the household and the corpse

characters.name: Father O'Rourke
characters.personality: religious, helpful, officiant in last rites
characters.lifespan: alive; called to anoint Father Flynn and arrange ecclesiastical matters

characters.name: The woman who washed him (washer)
characters.personality: practical, competent (handled the washing and laying-out)
characters.lifespan: alive; cited for helping with the corpse

characters.name: Telegram boy
characters.lifespan: alive; appears reading the funeral card

characters.name: Two poor women (onlookers)
characters.lifespan: alive; seen reading the card on the door
